title: Frequently Asked Questions
text:
Should the diagnostic code DS098C Let hovedtraume (GCS 14–15) be used according to the definition in the Scandinavian Neurotrauma Committee (SNC) guidelines?
No. The code should be applied to all patients with head trauma and a Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS) score of 14–15 at the time of admission, including those who would be classified as “minimal” according to the SNC guidelines.

Which patients belong in the category “øget blødningstendens”?
“Øget blødningstendens” refers to patients with alcohol abuse, liver disease, or bleeding disorders - not those receiving anticoagulant (AK) therapy, which is listed as a separate risk factor.

What if we cannot measure S100B?
All participating emergency departments can, as a general rule, measure S100B. If S100B is temporarily unavailable or more than 6 hours have passed since the injury, a CT scan should be performed (and the patient discharged if normal). Observation is only required if the CT scan is abnormal.

title: About
text:
Background
Minor head trauma accounts for a substantial proportion of emergency department visits globally. Approximately half of these patients undergo CT scanning to rule out intracranial haemorrhage requiring intervention. However, the vast majority of scans are without clinically relevant findings, and only 0-1% result in neurosurgical intervention. We aim to evaluate a new management algorithm developed by the Danish Choosing Wisely organization against the 2013 Scandinavian Neurotrauma Committee guidelines.
We hypothesize that the Choosing Wisely algorithm will reduce CT use by of 25-30% without compromising patient safety.


Aim
HEADWISE aims to compare a new Choosing Wisely recommendation with the current guidelines from the Scandinavian Neurotrauma Committee (SNC). The new recommendation is expected to reduce CT utilization by 25% without compromising patient safety


Methods
A stepped-wedge multicenter cluster-randomized non-inferiority trial including 10 Danish emergency departments, covering all five Danish regions.
Each department will serve as a cluster, initially following the Scandinavian guidelines before sequentially transitioning to the Choosing Wisely guidelines by random selection. Eligible patients are adults (>18 years) presenting within 24 hours of head trauma with a Glasgow Coma Scale score of 14-15 at admission.





The primary outcome is the proportion of patients undergoing neurosurgery. Secondary outcomes include mortality, length of hospital stay, CT scan rates, and economic impact. Data will be obtained from national healthcare registers and only data part of routine clinical documentation will be needed. Approximately 7000 patients will be included. For continuous outcomes, a linear mixed-effects model will be used. For binary outcomes, a logistic mixed-effects model will be used. Time-to-event analyses will assess mortality and other temporal endpoints.


Ethics
The study has been approved by the Danish Research Ethics Committees (case no.: 2403883, document no.: 3024022). A simplified informed consent model will be used: Patients will receive printed study information and may opt out of data use without affecting clinical management. Results will be disseminated through peer-reviewed publications and presentations at international scientific conferences.


Timeline
Ongoing retrospective data collection and analysis to determine power. Study start 2026.


Funding

title: Deploy Site
text:
# Render full site
quarto render .

# Only commit if there are changes
if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
  git add -f docs
  git commit -m "Site update: $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')"
  git push
else
  echo "No changes to commit"
fi

title: Protocol
text:
Study Overview








Item
Details




Study name
HEADWISE - Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficiently


Objective
Evaluate the diagnostic performance and safety profile of a new Choosing Wisely algorithm for MHT management, aiming to reduce CT overuse.


Study Sites
Aalborg, Aarhus, Esbjerg, Kolding, Aabenraa, Odense, Slagelse, Herlev, Bispebjerg, Hvidovre


Study Period
01.XX.202X – 01.XX.202X


Primary Endpoint
Proportion undergoing neurosurgery within 10 days of trauma


Secondary Endpoints
All-cause and cause-specific mortality
Readmission-free survival
Time from trauma to neurosurgery
Rate of intracranial haemorrhage
Duration of hospitalization and ICU admission if applicable
Number of CT scans, possible neurology admission, and neurorehabilitation
Economic consequences including elective CTs and sickness benefit


Follow-up
Three months





Roles and Responsibilities




Role
Person
Responsibilities




Principal Investigator
Rozh Al-Mashhadi
Overall study oversight, data quality


Local Investigator
See Team
Daily workflow management, staff training


Local Project Manager
See Team
Aid the local site investigator in quality assurance


Data Collector
Julie Bjørn Bay
Data extraction from registers


Clinician
Any doctor at admission site
Patient screening, patient information, guideline adherence, code assignment


Statistician

Data analysis plan

title: Practical protocol
text:
Study Overview





Study name
HEADWISE - Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficiently


Objective
Evaluate the diagnostic performance and safety profile of a new Choosing Wisely algorithm for MHT management, with the goal of reducing CT overuse in emergency settings.


Study Sites
Aalborg University Hospital, Aarhus University Hospital, Esbjerg Hospital, Kolding Hospital, Hospital Sønderjylland, Odense University Hospital, Slagelse Hospital, Herlev Hospital, Bispebjerg and Frederiksberg Hospital, and Amager and Hvidovre Hospital


Study Period
01.XX.202X - 01.XX.202X


Primary Endpoint
Proportion undergoing neurosurgery


Secondary Endpoints
Mortality, survival without readmission, proportion receiving CT of the brain, economic consequences, and related measures.











Roles and Responsibilities






Principal Investigator
Rozh Al-Mashhadi
Overall study oversight, data quality


Site Coordinator

Daily workflow management, staff training


Data Collector
Julie Bjørn Bay
Data extraction from registers


Clinician

Patient screening, guideline adherence


Statistician

Data analysis plan








Study Workflow
Step 1 – Patient Screening

Identify adult patients presenting with mild head trauma (GCS 14–15)
Apply inclusion/exclusion criteria
Hand eligible patients Patient Information

Step 2 – Guideline Assignment

Check which guideline is active at your site based on stepped-wedge schedule
Use flowchart to decide whether a CT scan is indicated

Step 3 – Data Collection

If patient is eligible assign the diagnosis code DS098C - Let hovedtraume (GCS 14-15)
If patient is not eligible use another diagnosis code
If patient want to opt out of data analysis, the patient should register this in REDCap or send us an email (see patient information)

title: HEADWISE
text:
Head Trauma Evaluation Algorithm for Deploying
Warranted Imaging Safely and Efficient



A multicenter cluster-randomized trial




Minor head trauma is common in emergency departments
Approximately half of patients undergo CT scanning
The vast majority of scans are without relevant findings
Only 0–1% result in treatment

We hypothesize
that choosing wisely can safely reduce CT use by 25–30%

title: Trial Procedures
text:
Local Visits
The Organizing Committee will visit the 10 participating departments ahead of trial start to inform thoroughly about the study. Posters and flyers will be distributed. The Organizing Committee will also visit the departments several times during the trial period, including at the time of transition.
